title: Business Breakfast | Stock/Share Market News | 12th May 2026 || TV5 Money Live

recommendations.note: Buy on any dip into 360-380; succession set up, returns to old glory over time
recommendations.detail: Kutumba Rao said to watch Biocon and buy it on any fall, whenever the stock comes into the Rs 360-380 band. He reasoned that the promoter has set the stage for succession and will clearly nominate a successor, so over a period Biocon will return to its old glory.

recommendations.note: His Dec investment idea; results today key to justify the sharp rally
recommendations.detail: Kutumba Rao said he has more interest in MTR, which rose very sharply recently, because they have been promoting it as an investment idea since December. He said its results today are key to see whether the rally was justified, so he flagged it to watch around the result.

recommendations.note: Terrific result, profit up over 200%, pan-India presence; might jump
recommendations.detail: Kutumba Rao noted Satin Creditcare, which he had mentioned earlier, delivered a terrific result with profit jumping over 200% and a pan-India presence. He said the stock might jump, framing it as part of staying invested in fundamentally sound, cash-rich stocks.

recommendations.note: His first choice for CDMO / overseas-exposure theme
recommendations.detail: When discussing CDMO and overseas-exposure ideas, Kutumba Rao said his first choice would be Laurus. He presented it ahead of his second choice (Sai Parenterals) as a preferred play on the CDMO story.

recommendations.note: Second choice CDMO play; recently listed, add on dips
recommendations.detail: Kutumba Rao named Sai Parenterals, a recently listed company, as his second choice, citing a good CDMO story. He advised putting it on the watch list and adding to it whenever dips come.

recommendations.note: EV/energy-transition theme; possible sustained mid-term rally
recommendations.detail: Kutumba Rao flagged that companies like JBM Auto and Olectra may have a sustained mid-term rally because of the emphasis the PM placed on the EV space. He said if the crude-oil crisis continues, demand will lean toward EV vehicles and EV public-transport buses, making it a theme to follow carefully.
